position_in_sequence: 2nd (after Copilot). To be delivered in a new chat session.
your_assignments: Q4 — Parameter provenance for every CL-05 constant
context_summary: Your CL-05 v2 attempt (G.25-G.31) was assessed in RE-023. Architecture accepted (~60-70% there), implementation flagged. This is the repair pass. The full briefing is SA_CL05_Collective_Rework_v1.0.json.
what_is_accepted_from_your_work: Adaptive gain scheduling off Keff_fill — correct structure
Multi-phase uniform splitting — provably optimal for convergence rate
Simplex projection after correction — standard, correct
51/49 veto — architecturally sound, threshold is policy
Operator equation dρ/dt = -K(t)·(ρ - ρ_ref) + correction terms — form is right
Backblaze Keff_fill diagnostic — genuine value-add, computable from real data

Q3_lyapunov.problem: Your Lyapunov function V(t) = α·(φ-φ*)² + B(t) where B(t) = γ(ln10 - H(p)) includes an entropy deficit term the controller does not steer. Your own simulation showed V increasing from 0.2613 to 0.2856 and stabilizing permanently. Theorem G.31 claims V → 0. The simulation disproves the theorem.
Q3_lyapunov.instruction: Drop B(t). Use V = (φ - φ*)² + λ·TV(ρ, ρ_ref)². Prove V̇ < 0 under your operator equation. Then run your simulation again and verify proof matches simulation. If V converges to a positive value, state what it converges to and why. Honesty over elegance.
Q3_lyapunov.what_clean_looks_like: V̇ = 2(φ-φ*)·φ̇ + 2λ·TV·d(TV)/dt. Substitute your operator equation for φ̇ and the implied TV dynamics. Show this is negative (or negative semi-definite with characterization of the invariant set).

Q4_parameters.problem: γ=0.3, δ=0.2, gain reference ln10, contraction constants 0.12 and 0.06 — none derived, none explained.
Q4_parameters.instruction: For each parameter in your CL-05 operator: state whether derived or tuning. For tuning parameters: state range, monotonicity (what happens as it increases), and what breaks at boundaries. The PLL discipline: if it is not constrained by the system's physics, say so and give the sensitivity.
Q4_parameters.example_of_good_answer: γ ∈ [0.1, 0.5]: controls relative weight of fill-ratio vs composition tracking. Higher γ prioritizes φ convergence, lower γ prioritizes ρ convergence. At γ < 0.1 the fill-ratio signal is effectively ignored; at γ > 0.5 composition corrections are too slow for practical convergence in T < 20 time steps.

what_copilot_delivered_for_context.amplifier_boundary: Amplifier is forward path (static structural map), CL-05 is feedback path (dynamic response). CL-05 is external to the amplifier quadruple A=(D,C,Keff,f).
what_copilot_delivered_for_context.a_i_definition: a_i = 1 - average TV between predicted and realized carriers. Concrete, PLL-compliant.
what_copilot_delivered_for_context.implication_for_cl05: CL-05 reads the amplifier's output (or its per-domain components) and applies correction. The gain schedule K(t) = f(Keff_fill(t)) is CL-05's own machinery, not part of the amplifier.

what_not_to_do: Do not extend the theorem tower — no new G.32+, no partition theory, no Ramanujan congruences.
Do not build on G.23 (partition multiplicity) — it is flagged and stripped from CL-05 dependency.
Do not produce decorative domain applications (Ramsar case studies from partition counts, etc.).
Do not claim ADVANCE — this is a repair pass, not a review verdict.
If your simulation contradicts your proof, fix the proof. Do not ignore the simulation.
dead_items: G.23 (partition multiplicity W(φ) = p(n)) — arbitrary N, unconstrained partitions
G.24 (Ramsar partition multiplicity) — derivative of G.23
Ramanujan congruences as CL-03 observables — mathematically true, operationally useless
Riemann-HUF bridge — sharing a toolkit is not a connection
deliverable: A reworked CL-05 with: (1) Lyapunov function V = (φ-φ*)² + λ·TV² with proved V̇ < 0, (2) simulation verifying the proof, (3) every parameter labeled as derived or tuning with range/sensitivity, (4) no G.23 dependency, (5) gain schedule K(t) = f(Keff_fill) with f specified (linear, log, or sigmoid) and justified.
